clues: In the 19th century, this mysterious illness swept through Europe and the US called 'chlorosis' or the 'green sickness' as affected individuals became fatigued, breathless and developed a pale, slightly greenish complexion

Ancient Greek and Roman physicians observed that wounded war veterans suffered from severe muscular weakness, which we now recognize as this disease. To treat it, soldiers and sufferers would drink water in which a sword had rusted
In addition to those symptoms this can also cause pica, restless legs, koilonychia, and atrophic glossitis

The earliest written descriptions of this disease are from ancient China sometime during the third century with swelling, numbness, and weakness of the feet. The illness was later found to be linked to white rice consumption, particularly after the introduction of rice mills

Breastfed babies with affected mothers can develop an aphonic version where their cries transition from a hoarse sound to completely soundless due to damage to the laryngeal nerve. If left untreated, they can develop heart failure, nystagmus and seizures
You could be wet or dry. This disease can be caused by diet, gastric bypass, hyperemesis gravidarum, and chronic alcoholism

The very first paper on this condition was published in 1794 by an English scientist, John Dalton, who had the condition himself. Because of his pioneering work, the condition was called Daltonism in Europe for decades

In the late 19th century, this condition became recognized as a public safety issue when a rail operator misread a signal leading to a fatal accident. This led to the development of tests such as the Wool test and later the Ishihara Test
Facebook's iconic color scheme exists because the founder, Mark Zuckerberg, has this condition. Surprisingly, 95% of all affected individuals are male.
The first recorded case of this was in 1586 by an Italian physician who described a young man who developed this condition after eating an egg
Sir William Osler treated a young woman with swelling and abdominal pain and discovered 28 affected family members with two tragic deaths from asphyxiation
It wasn't until 1963 that researchers discovered patients were missing the C1 inhibitor, which allowed bradykinin to flood the system and rapidly leak fluid into tissues
Some speculate that Harriet Tubman suffered from this disease after a severe traumatic brain injury and this may have been the source of her visions from God that directed her routes to freedom
You may not know, but this is typically an autoimmune disease in humans where the body attacks and destroys neurons in the brain that produce hypocretin

This condition is most prominently studied in dogs where they might suddenly enter REM sleep or lose muscle tone when playing or excited. Studying dogs helped scientists identify that hypocretin regulates wakefulness
